title: Q2 renewal forecast closes 1.7% under commit
anchor: Forecast ARR at $1.8M versus actual ARR of $1.77M, the tightest variance in trailing four quarters.
body: Forecast ARR at $1.8M versus actual ARR of $1.77M, the tightest variance in trailing four quarters. Trailing four-quarter forecast variance averaged 8.2%.
trace: Q2 forecast accuracy at 1.7% variance represents significant improvement from the 8.2% four-quarter average.

title: Mid-market NRR hits 112% in Q2
anchor: Mid-market cohort expansion at 112% versus trailing three-quarter average of 104.8%.
connect: Enterprise NRR sits at 106%; small-business at 102%.
body: Mid-market cohort expansion at 112% versus trailing three-quarter average of 104.8%. Enterprise NRR sits at 106%; small-business at 102%.
trace: Mid-market segment showing strongest expansion performance, 7 points above trailing average.

title: Multi-product customers expand at 124% rate
anchor: Multi-product accounts generate 124% NRR versus single-product accounts at 102%.
connect: Multi-product share of customer base sits at 38.3%.
body: Multi-product accounts generate 124% NRR versus single-product accounts at 102%. Multi-product share of customer base sits at 38.3%.
trace: 22-point NRR gap between multi-product and single-product customers shows product breadth as expansion driver.

title: At-risk ARR pool shrinks to $220K in April
anchor: At-risk pool sits at $220K across 3 accounts versus March's $310K across 7 accounts.
connect: Top-20-ARR accounts in at-risk status dropped from 5 to 2.
body: At-risk pool sits at $220K across 3 accounts versus March's $310K across 7 accounts. Top-20-ARR accounts in at-risk status dropped from 5 to 2.
trace: Month-over-month risk pool improvement with 4 accounts resolved and no new additions.

title: Mid-market time to first value compresses to 23 days
anchor: Q2 mid-market TTFV at 23 days versus Q1's 38 days across similar cohort size.
connect: Q1 cohort under-30-day TTFV renewed at 91% versus over-60-day at 74%.
body: Q2 mid-market TTFV at 23 days versus Q1's 38 days across similar cohort size. Q1 cohort under-30-day TTFV renewed at 91% versus over-60-day at 74%.
trace: TTFV improvement positions Q2 cohort for stronger retention based on Q1 correlation patterns.

title: Health-review expansion opportunities reach 83% acceptance
anchor: Customer health review expansion opportunities at 83% sales acceptance in March versus outbound at 41%.
connect: April health reviews generated 8 opportunities totaling $340K.
body: Customer health review expansion opportunities at 83% sales acceptance in March versus outbound at 41%. April health reviews generated 8 opportunities totaling $340K.
trace: Health review channel showing 2x acceptance rate versus outbound prospecting for expansion opportunities.

title: Atlas Insights adoption drives mid-market expansion
anchor: Mid-market customers running Atlas Insights expanded at 75% rate across 18 of 24 accounts.
connect: Mid-market NRR overall sits at 112% for Q2.
body: Mid-market customers running Atlas Insights expanded at 75% rate across 18 of 24 accounts. Mid-market NRR overall sits at 112% for Q2.
trace: Atlas Insights feature showing strong correlation with expansion behavior in mid-market segment.

title: License utilization above 80% jumps to 22 mid-market accounts
anchor: Mid-market customers above 80% license utilization at 22 in Q2 versus 14 in Q1.
connect: Mid-market NRR expanded from trailing average of 104.8% to 112%.
body: Mid-market customers above 80% license utilization at 22 in Q2 versus 14 in Q1. Mid-market NRR expanded from trailing average of 104.8% to 112%.
trace: License utilization increase correlating with mid-market expansion performance.

title: High-touch tier retention runs 14 points above tech-touch
anchor: High-touch coverage GRR at 96% versus tech-touch at 82% in Q2.
connect: High-touch covers 18.3% of customer base across top-ARR accounts.
body: High-touch coverage GRR at 96% versus tech-touch at 82% in Q2. High-touch covers 18.3% of customer base across top-ARR accounts.
trace: Coverage tier showing significant retention divergence with 14-point GRR spread.

title: Green health scores expand to 78% of mid-market book
anchor: Mid-market health distribution at 78% green versus Q1's 71% across the same 55-account cohort.
connect: Q1 green-health customers renewed at 95% versus yellow-health at 75%.
body: Mid-market health distribution at 78% green versus Q1's 71% across the same 55-account cohort. Q1 green-health customers renewed at 95% versus yellow-health at 75%.
trace: Health score improvement in mid-market segment with 7-point increase in green distribution.

title: New customer 90-day retention climbs to 88%
anchor: Q1 2026 new-customer cohort 90-day retention at 88% versus Q4 2025 at 83%.
connect: TTFV compressed from 38 days in Q4 to 26 days in Q1.
body: Q1 2026 new-customer cohort 90-day retention at 88% versus Q4 2025 at 83%. TTFV compressed from 38 days in Q4 to 26 days in Q1.
trace: Early retention improvement correlating with faster time to first value across new customer cohorts.

title: Beacon Logistics signs $280K renewal 3 months early
anchor: Beacon Logistics renewal signed April 21st versus July 15th contract end date.
connect: April enterprise renewals include 3 with executive sponsor depth added during Q1.
body: Beacon Logistics renewal signed April 21st versus July 15th contract end date. April enterprise renewals include 3 with executive sponsor depth added during Q1.
trace: Early renewal pattern emerging in enterprise accounts with strengthened executive relationships.

title: Multi-product NRR acceleration widens to 8 points
anchor: Multi-product customers expand from 116% in Q1 to 124% in Q2.
connect: Single-product customers hold steady at 102% across both quarters.
body: Multi-product customers expand from 116% in Q1 to 124% in Q2. Single-product customers hold steady at 102% across both quarters.
trace: Multi-product customer expansion accelerating while single-product remains flat, widening the expansion differential.

title: Executive sponsor reviews scheduled for 2 top-ARR accounts
anchor: Top-20-ARR accounts in at-risk status have executive sponsor review on calendar this week.
connect: At-risk pool concentration in top-20 accounts dropped from 5 to 2 since March.
body: Top-20-ARR accounts in at-risk status have executive sponsor review on calendar this week. At-risk pool concentration in top-20 accounts dropped from 5 to 2 since March.
trace: Executive escalation process active for remaining high-value at-risk accounts.

title: Tech-touch retention improves 4 points quarter over quarter
anchor: Tech-touch coverage GRR at 82% in Q2 versus 86% in Q1.
connect: Tech-touch covers 98 accounts across the customer base.
body: Tech-touch coverage GRR at 82% in Q2 versus 86% in Q1. Tech-touch covers 98 accounts across the customer base.
trace: Tech-touch tier showing retention decline despite overall portfolio improvement.

title: Custom Permissions launch window opens June 15th
anchor: Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15th for enterprise and multi-product accounts.
connect: Multi-product customers currently expand at 124% NRR.
body: Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15th for enterprise and multi-product accounts. Multi-product customers currently expand at 124% NRR.
trace: Product launch timing aligns with strong multi-product expansion performance.

title: Small-business GRR sits at 86% with minimal expansion
anchor: Small-business cohort GRR at 86% with NRR at 102%.
connect: Mid-market GRR at 91% generates 112% NRR in Q2.
body: Small-business cohort GRR at 86% with NRR at 102%. Mid-market GRR at 91% generates 112% NRR in Q2.
trace: Small-business segment showing retention challenges with limited expansion opportunity.

title: Enterprise segment retention holds at 94% with modest expansion
anchor: Enterprise GRR at 94% generates 106% NRR across the $432K renewing book.
connect: Enterprise covers 22 high-touch accounts at 18.3% of customer base.
body: Enterprise GRR at 94% generates 106% NRR across the $432K renewing book. Enterprise covers 22 high-touch accounts at 18.3% of customer base.
trace: Enterprise segment showing stable retention with conservative expansion patterns.
